== '''External Links''' == * [https://www.jinja-honcho.or.jp/ Shrine Association of Japan] * [[:ja:天羽槌雄神|Japanese Wikipedia: Amano Hachio-no-Kami]] {{Shizuri Faith}}{{Shinto-stub}} [[fr:Utilisateur:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi]]

{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}}{{Infobox deity |image = Bentenjima on Inasa Beach.jpg|right|thumb|250x250px| caption = Inasa Beach ([[:ja:稲佐の浜|稲佐の浜]] ''Inasa-no-hama'') in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|lt=Izumo|en}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en}}, where the myth places Ōkuninushi's meeting with the messengers of heaven and his surrender to them.|cult_centre={{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en}} {{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shizumi Shrine }} {{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社}}|type=Shinto|name=Takehazuchi|script_name=天羽槌雄神|other_names=Ama no Hazuchi|father={{ill|Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|simple|user:immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto}}}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr}} (天羽槌雄神){{efn|also read as Amenohazuchi}} is the Japanese goddess of weaving<ref name="Nihongi6970">{{cite wikisource|author=Aston|date=1896|Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|chapter=Book II|pages=69–70}}</ref> Also known as Shizuri.<ref name="mizue-2">{{Cite web |last=Mizue |first=Mori |title=Shizuri |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9154 |website={{ill|Kokugakuin University|en}} Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>
She is believed to be a fifth generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en}} most likely through [[Sukunabikona]] due to the other associations of him.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=天羽槌雄神（あめのはづちのおのかみ） ・建葉槌命（たけはづちのみこと）／日本の神々の話 : 四季歩のつれづれ|url=http://tamtom.blog44.fc2.com/blog-entry-2646.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=tamtom.blog44.fc2.com}}</ref>
She decorated the streamers on the [[Masakaki]] tree used to lure out [[Amaterasu]] from the cave.<ref>{{Cite web|title=神々の庭：天羽槌雄神 (あめのはずちおのかみ)|url=http://shinto.fugarden.com/gosaishin_amenohazutio.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=shinto.fugarden.com}}</ref>
She is a warrior who vanquished the rebellious star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en}} during the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en}}.<ref name="宇治谷 1988">{{Harvnb|宇治谷|1988}} 『日本書紀』上 p.64, 58</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /><ref name="mizue-2" /> [[ja:天羽槌雄神]][[en:draft:Takehazuchi]]
Appended to the two passages of the subjugation of the earth in the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]] is the mention of a star deity named {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en}} who resisted till the end, and whom {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr}} were particularly eager to vanquish. The latter passage states that the being who subdued the star god, referred to as {{Nihongo|''Iwai no nushi''|斎の大人}} is enshrined at Katori, hinting that it might be {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr}}.<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h98EAAAAMAAJ|title=和漢三才図会|author=寺島良安|author2=島田勇雄, 樋口元巳|publisher=平凡社|year=1985|isbn=9784582804478|volume=10|page=71}} subscribes to this view</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /> However, the earlier passage says a god named {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft||fr}} was the vanquisher of the star god.<ref name="宇治谷 1988" /><ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-03-20|title=Hoshinokamikakaseo {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240320033035/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9400|access-date=2024-03-20|website=web.archive.org}}</ref>
After Ōkuninushi's assent and withdrawal, the two messengers proceeded to destroy everyone and everything who refused to submit to their authority. They then send the god of weaving, Takehazuchi (建葉槌), to subdue the star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|lt=Kagaseo|en}} (香香背男), the last remaining rebel against Takamagahara (''Nihon Shoki'').<ref name="Nihongi6970" /> After the resistance was gone the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en}} could begin.<ref name="mizue-2" />
It is believed it She may either have also been a war goddess based on the "take" in her name analogous to {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr}} or that She wove the stars into fabric, taming them.<ref name=":0" />
The {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en}} identifies him with Amenohatsuchio the ancestor of the Shizuri clan who did weaving.<ref name="mizue-2" />
At {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|4=fr|5=Shitori-jinja}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr}} are worshipped as weaving gods<ref name="mizue-2" />.
== Overview ==
Takehazuchi no Kami is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}'' as ''Takehazuchi no Kami''. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', she is referred to as {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命}} and {{ill|Shidori deity|ja|倭文神}}. In the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'', she is called {{ill|Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波知命}} in the Yamashiro Province section and {{ill|Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja|天破命}} in another entry. The ''Anwa Province Imbe Family Genealogy'' (安房国忌部家系), which includes the "Ibe no Sukune Honkeicho" (齋部宿祢本系帳), records her name as {{ill|Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷雄命}} and also lists {{ill|Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武羽槌命}} and {{ill|Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波豆知命}} as alternative names.<ref>{{cite book|和書 |title=安房国忌部家系|chapter=齋部宿祢本系帳|section=天羽雷雄命|doi=10.11501/2538213|url={{NDLDC|2538213/55}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション}})</ref>
She is said to have woven sacred cloth (''aya'') to lure {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} out of the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸}}. The cloth she wove is called {{ill|Shidori cloth|ja|倭文布}} or simply ''Shidori'' (倭文), a term referring to a type of woven fabric. Other weaving deities include {{ill|Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja|栲幡千々姫命}} and {{ill|Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}, but Takehazuchi no Kami is revered as the '''supreme ancestor of weaving'''. She is also the '''ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}'''.
She is worshipped as the '''goddess of weaving''' in '''{{ill|Shidori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}''', '''{{ill|Shizuka Shrine|ja|静神社}}''', '''Hattori Shrines''', and others throughout Japan.
== Genealogy ==
In ''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}'', she is called the '''far ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}'''. In ''{{ill|Sendai Kujihongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', she is described as the '''distant ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja|倭文造}}'''.
== Legends and Deeds ==
Takehazuchi no Kami appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' under the name {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命}}. She is said to have '''subjugated the star deity''' '''{{ill|Amanomikaboshi|ja|天津甕星}} (also known as Kagaseo no Kami)''', whom '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|武甕槌命}}''' and '''{{ill|Futsunushi-no-Kami|ja|経津主神}}''' were unable to pacify.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=植松安|date=1920-06|title=仮名日本書紀|volume=上巻|pages=87–88|publisher=大同館書店|url={{NDLDC|961006/117}} |doi=10.11501/961006 }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション}})</ref>

She decorated the streamers on the [[Masakaki]] tree used to lure out [[Amaterasu]] from the cave.<ref>{{Cite web|title=神々の庭：天羽槌雄神 (あめのはずちおのかみ)|url=http://shinto.fugarden.com/gosaishin_amenohazutio.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=shinto.fugarden.com}}</ref>
She is a warrior who vanquished the rebellious star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en}} during the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en}}.<ref name="宇治谷 1988">{{Harvnb|宇治谷|1988}} 『日本書紀』上 p.64, 58</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /><ref name="mizue-2" /> [[ja:天羽槌雄神]][[en:draft:Takehazuchi]]
Appended to the two passages of the subjugation of the earth in the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]] is the mention of a star deity named {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en}} who resisted till the end, and whom {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr}} were particularly eager to vanquish. The latter passage states that the being who subdued the star god, referred to as {{Nihongo|''Iwai no nushi''|斎の大人}} is enshrined at Katori, hinting that it might be {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr}}.<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h98EAAAAMAAJ|title=和漢三才図会|author=寺島良安|author2=島田勇雄, 樋口元巳|publisher=平凡社|year=1985|isbn=9784582804478|volume=10|page=71}} subscribes to this view</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /> However, the earlier passage says a god named {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft||fr}} was the vanquisher of the star god.<ref name="宇治谷 1988" /><ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-03-20|title=Hoshinokamikakaseo {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240320033035/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9400|access-date=2024-03-20|website=web.archive.org}}</ref>
After Ōkuninushi's assent and withdrawal, the two messengers proceeded to destroy everyone and everything who refused to submit to their authority. They then send the god of weaving, Takehazuchi (建葉槌), to subdue the star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|lt=Kagaseo|en}} (香香背男), the last remaining rebel against Takamagahara (''Nihon Shoki'').<ref name="Nihongi6970" /> After the resistance was gone the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en}} could begin.<ref name="mizue-2" />
It is believed it She may either have also been a war goddess based on the "take" in her name analogous to {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr}} or that She wove the stars into fabric, taming them.<ref name=":0" />
The {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en}} identifies him with Amenohatsuchio the ancestor of the Shizuri clan who did weaving.<ref name="mizue-2" />
At {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|4=fr|5=Shitori-jinja}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr}} are worshipped as weaving gods<ref name="mizue-2" />.
== Overview ==
Takehazuchi no Kami is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}'' as ''Takehazuchi no Kami''. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', she is referred to as {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命}} and {{ill|Shidori deity|ja|倭文神}}. In the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'', she is called {{ill|Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波知命}} in the Yamashiro Province section and {{ill|Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja|天破命}} in another entry. The ''Anwa Province Imbe Family Genealogy'' (安房国忌部家系), which includes the "Ibe no Sukune Honkeicho" (齋部宿祢本系帳), records her name as {{ill|Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷雄命}} and also lists {{ill|Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武羽槌命}} and {{ill|Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波豆知命}} as alternative names.<ref>{{cite book|和書 |title=安房国忌部家系|chapter=齋部宿祢本系帳|section=天羽雷雄命|doi=10.11501/2538213|url={{NDLDC|2538213/55}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション}})</ref>
She is said to have woven sacred cloth (''aya'') to lure {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} out of the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸}}. The cloth she wove is called {{ill|Shidori cloth|ja|倭文布}} or simply ''Shidori'' (倭文), a term referring to a type of woven fabric. Other weaving deities include {{ill|Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja|栲幡千々姫命}} and {{ill|Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}, but Takehazuchi no Kami is revered as the '''supreme ancestor of weaving'''. She is also the '''ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}'''.
She is worshipped as the '''goddess of weaving''' in '''{{ill|Shidori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}''', '''{{ill|Shizuka Shrine|ja|静神社}}''', '''Hattori Shrines''', and others throughout Japan.
== Genealogy ==
In ''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}'', she is called the '''far ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}'''. In ''{{ill|Sendai Kujihongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', she is described as the '''distant ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja|倭文造}}'''.
== Legends and Deeds ==
Takehazuchi no Kami appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' under the name {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命}}. She is said to have '''subjugated the star deity''' '''{{ill|Amanomikaboshi|ja|天津甕星}} (also known as Kagaseo no Kami)''', whom '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|武甕槌命}}''' and '''{{ill|Futsunushi-no-Kami|ja|経津主神}}''' were unable to pacify.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=植松安|date=1920-06|title=仮名日本書紀|volume=上巻|pages=87–88|publisher=大同館書店|url={{NDLDC|961006/117}} |doi=10.11501/961006 }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション}})</ref>
==Descendant clans==
{{ill|Nagahata clan|ja|長幡部連}}, {{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}, {{ill|Tanabe clan|ja|田辺宿禰}}, {{ill|Takehara clan|ja|竹原氏}}, {{ill|Okura clan|ja|巨椋連}}, {{ill|Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja|大椋置始連}}, {{ill|Imaki clan|ja|今木連}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, {{ill|Tame clan|ja|多米連}}, {{ill|Minu clan|ja|美努連}} ({{ill|Minokuni Rulers|ja|三野県主}}), {{ill|Tottoribe clan|ja|鳥取部連}}, {{ill|Jingu clan|ja|神宮部造}}, {{ill|Agatanoinukai clan|ja|県犬養氏}}
== Shrines ==<!--== Content from [[Shitori Shrine (Tottori)]] == --> {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr}} is believed to be the ancestor of the {{Nihongo|Shitori clan|倭文氏}} of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en}}<ref name="Yoshiki" />.<ref name="mizue-2" />
However, the legends of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en}} are all centered around {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr}}, whom the shrine legend claims arrived in this location from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo|en}} by boat. She established her residence on the mountain behind this shrine, and after her death, she was buried in a ''{{ill|kofun|en}}'' constructed in the shrine's precincts.<ref name="Yoshiki">{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}}</ref> == See Also ==

revision.comment: added [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]
revision.text:
{{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}}{{Infobox deity |image = Bentenjima on Inasa Beach.jpg|right|thumb|250x250px| caption = {{ill|Inasa Beach|ja|稲佐浜}} ([[:ja:稲佐の浜|稲佐の浜]] ''Inasa-no-hama'') in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|lt=Izumo|en}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en}}, where the myth places Ōkuninushi's meeting with the messengers of heaven and his surrender to them.|cult_centre={{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en}} {{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shizumi Shrine }} {{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社}}|type=Shinto|name=Takehazuchi|script_name=天羽槌雄神|other_names=Ama no Hazuchi|father={{ill|Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|simple|user:immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto}}}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr}} (天羽槌雄神){{efn|also read as Amenohazuchi}} is the Japanese goddess of weaving<ref name="Nihongi6970">{{cite wikisource|author=Aston|date=1896|Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|chapter=Book II|pages=69–70}}</ref> Also known as Shizuri.<ref name="mizue-2">{{Cite web |last=Mizue |first=Mori |title=Shizuri |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9154 |website={{ill|Kokugakuin University|en}} Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>
She is believed to be a fifth generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en}} most likely through [[Sukunabikona]] due to the other associations of him.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=天羽槌雄神（あめのはづちのおのかみ） ・建葉槌命（たけはづちのみこと）／日本の神々の話 : 四季歩のつれづれ|url=http://tamtom.blog44.fc2.com/blog-entry-2646.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=tamtom.blog44.fc2.com}}</ref>
She decorated the streamers on the [[Masakaki]] tree used to lure out [[Amaterasu]] from the cave.<ref>{{Cite web|title=神々の庭：天羽槌雄神 (あめのはずちおのかみ)|url=http://shinto.fugarden.com/gosaishin_amenohazutio.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=shinto.fugarden.com}}</ref>
She is a warrior who vanquished the rebellious star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en}} during the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en}}.<ref name="宇治谷 1988">{{Harvnb|宇治谷|1988}} 『日本書紀』上 p.64, 58</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /><ref name="mizue-2" /> [[ja:天羽槌雄神]][[en:draft:Takehazuchi]]
Appended to the two passages of the subjugation of the earth in the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]] is the mention of a star deity named {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en}} who resisted till the end, and whom {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr}} were particularly eager to vanquish. The latter passage states that the being who subdued the star god, referred to as {{Nihongo|''Iwai no nushi''|斎の大人}} is enshrined at Katori, hinting that it might be {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr}}.<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h98EAAAAMAAJ|title=和漢三才図会|author=寺島良安|author2=島田勇雄, 樋口元巳|publisher=平凡社|year=1985|isbn=9784582804478|volume=10|page=71}} subscribes to this view</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /> However, the earlier passage says a god named {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft||fr}} was the vanquisher of the star god.<ref name="宇治谷 1988" /><ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-03-20|title=Hoshinokamikakaseo {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240320033035/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9400|access-date=2024-03-20|website=web.archive.org}}</ref>
After Ōkuninushi's assent and withdrawal, the two messengers proceeded to destroy everyone and everything who refused to submit to their authority. They then send the god of weaving, Takehazuchi (建葉槌), to subdue the star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|lt=Kagaseo|en}} (香香背男), the last remaining rebel against Takamagahara (''Nihon Shoki'').<ref name="Nihongi6970" /> After the resistance was gone the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en}} could begin.<ref name="mizue-2" />
It is believed it She may either have also been a war goddess based on the "take" in her name analogous to {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr}} or that She wove the stars into fabric, taming them.<ref name=":0" />
The {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en}} identifies him with Amenohatsuchio the ancestor of the Shizuri clan who did weaving.<ref name="mizue-2" />
At {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|4=fr|5=Shitori-jinja}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr}} are worshipped as weaving gods<ref name="mizue-2" />.
== Overview ==
Takehazuchi no Kami is mentioned in the ''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}'' as ''Takehazuchi no Kami''. In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'', she is referred to as {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命}} and {{ill|Shidori deity|ja|倭文神}}. In the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'', she is called {{ill|Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波知命}} in the Yamashiro Province section and {{ill|Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja|天破命}} in another entry. The ''Anwa Province Imbe Family Genealogy'' (安房国忌部家系), which includes the "Ibe no Sukune Honkeicho" (齋部宿祢本系帳), records her name as {{ill|Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷雄命}} and also lists {{ill|Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武羽槌命}} and {{ill|Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波豆知命}} as alternative names.<ref>{{cite book|和書 |title=安房国忌部家系|chapter=齋部宿祢本系帳|section=天羽雷雄命|doi=10.11501/2538213|url={{NDLDC|2538213/55}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション}})</ref>
She is said to have woven sacred cloth (''aya'') to lure {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} out of the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸}}. The cloth she wove is called {{ill|Shidori cloth|ja|倭文布}} or simply ''Shidori'' (倭文), a term referring to a type of woven fabric. Other weaving deities include {{ill|Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja|栲幡千々姫命}} and {{ill|Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}, but Takehazuchi no Kami is revered as the '''supreme ancestor of weaving'''. She is also the '''ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}'''.
She is worshipped as the '''goddess of weaving''' in '''{{ill|Shidori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}}''', '''{{ill|Shizuka Shrine|ja|静神社}}''', '''Hattori Shrines''', and others throughout Japan.
== Genealogy ==
In ''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}'', she is called the '''far ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}'''. In ''{{ill|Sendai Kujihongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'', she is described as the '''distant ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja|倭文造}}'''.
== Legends and Deeds ==
Takehazuchi no Kami appears in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' under the name {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命}}. She is said to have '''subjugated the star deity''' '''{{ill|Amanomikaboshi|ja|天津甕星}} (also known as Kagaseo no Kami)''', whom '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|武甕槌命}}''' and '''{{ill|Futsunushi-no-Kami|ja|経津主神}}''' were unable to pacify.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=植松安|date=1920-06|title=仮名日本書紀|volume=上巻|pages=87–88|publisher=大同館書店|url={{NDLDC|961006/117}} |doi=10.11501/961006 }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション}})</ref>
==Descendant clans==
{{ill|Nagahata clan|ja|長幡部連}}, {{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}, {{ill|Tanabe clan|ja|田辺宿禰}}, {{ill|Takehara clan|ja|竹原氏}}, {{ill|Okura clan|ja|巨椋連}}, {{ill|Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja|大椋置始連}}, {{ill|Imaki clan|ja|今木連}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, {{ill|Tame clan|ja|多米連}}, {{ill|Minu clan|ja|美努連}} ({{ill|Minokuni Rulers|ja|三野県主}}), {{ill|Tottoribe clan|ja|鳥取部連}}, {{ill|Jingu clan|ja|神宮部造}}, {{ill|Agatanoinukai clan|ja|県犬養氏}}
== Shrines ==<!--== Content from [[Shitori Shrine (Tottori)]] == --> {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr}} is believed to be the ancestor of the {{Nihongo|Shitori clan|倭文氏}} of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en}}<ref name="Yoshiki" />.<ref name="mizue-2" />
However, the legends of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en}} are all centered around {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr}}, whom the shrine legend claims arrived in this location from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo|en}} by boat. She established her residence on the mountain behind this shrine, and after her death, she was buried in a ''{{ill|kofun|en}}'' constructed in the shrine's precincts.<ref name="Yoshiki">{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}}</ref> == See Also ==
* {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * ''{{ill|Your Name|ja|君の名は。}}'' (2016 anime film), where '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''{{ill|Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|倭文神 建葉槌命}}'''. * {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr}} * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:Textile_production_in_Japan|Textile production in Japan]] * [[:en:Shinto_cosmology|Shinto cosmology]] * [[:en:Weaving_in_mythology|Weaving in mythology]]

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 天羽槌雄神 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 世代名 = | 先代 = | 次代 = | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 天羽槌雄神 | 別名 = 建葉槌命、止与波知命、天破命、天羽雷雄命、武羽槌命、止与波豆知命 など | 別称 = <!-- 天孫、山幸彦など神名でない称号 -->倭文神 | 神階 = | 神格 = [[機織]] | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 兄弟姉妹 = | 子 = | 神社 = [[倭文神社]]、[[静神社]]、[[葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] | 御利益 = <!-- 五穀豊穣、安産、学問など --> | 記紀 = | 関連氏族 = 長幡部連、[[倭文氏|倭文連]]、田辺宿禰、竹原氏、巨椋連、大椋置始連、今木連、[[弓削氏|弓削連]]、多米連、美努連（三野県主）、鳥取部連、神宮部造、[[県犬養氏|県犬養連]] }} '''天羽槌雄神'''（あまのはづちおのかみ）は、[[日本神話]]の女[[神 (神道)|神]]である。
== 概要 == 『[[古語拾遺]]』において'''天羽槌雄神'''とされるほか、『[[日本書紀]]』では'''建葉槌命'''（たけはづちのみこと）、'''倭文神'''（しとりがみ）とされる。また、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』の山城国神別 天神 巨椋連の条では'''止与波知命'''（とよはちのみこと）、山城国神別 天神 神宮部造の条では'''天破命'''（あまのはのみこと）、『安房国忌部家系』に収録されている「齋部宿祢本系帳」<ref>{{cite book|和書 |title=安房国忌部家系|chapter=齋部宿祢本系帳|section=天羽雷雄命|doi=10.11501/2538213|url={{NDLDC|2538213/55}} }}（[[国立国会図書館#国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]]）</ref>では'''天羽雷雄命'''（あまのはづちおのみこと）、別名、'''武羽槌命'''（たけはづちのみこと）、'''止与波豆知命'''（とよはづちのみこと）とある。
[[天照大神]]を[[天の岩戸]]から誘い出すために、文布（あや）を織ったとされる。文布は倭文布とも倭文とも書き、「シドリ」また「シヅリ」という織物である。同じ織物の神では[[栲幡千々姫命]]、[[天棚機姫神|天棚機姫命]]が挙げられるが、天羽槌雄神は機織りの祖神とされている。また倭文（しどり）氏の遠祖でもある。
信仰としてはどちらの名でも織物の神、機織の神として信仰され、全国の[[倭文神社]]、[[静神社]]、服部神社などで祀られている。
== 系譜 == 『古語拾遺』神代段では「倭文遠祖」、『先代旧事本紀』神祇本紀では「倭文造遠祖」と書かれる。
== 事績 == 天羽槌雄神は建葉槌命の名で『[[日本書紀]]』に登場した倭文神で、[[経津主神]]・[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌命]]では服従しなかった星神の[[天津甕星|香香背男]]（かがせお）を服従させた神とされる<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=植松安|date=1920-06|title=仮名日本書紀|volume=上巻|pafes=87–88|publisher=大同館書店|url={{NDLDC|961006/117}} |doi=10.11501/961006 }}（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）</ref>。
== 考察 == 織物の神が、何故星の神を服従させる事が出来たのか、色々諸説ある内の説を挙げるとすると、
*1つ目は、建葉槌命が武神だったとする説。建葉槌命の「建」は「武」、「葉」は「刃」と読み替えると武刃槌となり、まさに武神らしい名と受けとれるからといわれる{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。 **この説の裏付けとして、武葉槌命を祀る大甕倭文神社（茨城県日立市）の『大甕倭文神宮縁起』からも武神であるむねを窺わせる内容が記されている{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。 *2つ目は、織物の中に星を織り込んでしまって、星の神を織物の中に封印したとする説。これは、太陽が沈んでも空に星が残っている事を、どうにか出来ないものかと考えた上での苦肉の策だとされる{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。 **日本書紀第九段一書（二）に「天に悪しき神有り。名を天津甕星（あまつみかほし）またの名を天香香背男（あまのかかせお）と曰う。請う、先ず此の神を誅し、然る後に下りて葦原中國をはらわん」。是の時に齋主（いわい）の神を齋之大人（いわいのうし）ともうす。とあり、日本書紀第九段本文と似た記述がある。これにより齋之大人＝建葉槌命とみられ、齋主（祭祀）で征服したとある{{要出典|date=2023年3月}}ので上記の行為を齋主で行うことにより星神香香背男＝天津甕星を征服したという説である。
他にも、香香背男側にいた建葉槌命を懐柔し味方に付け、内側から崩壊させた。などの説もある{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の神の一覧]] * [[君の名は。]] [[2016年]]のアニメーション映画。作中で登場する宮水神社が倭文神建葉槌命を祀っているという設定。
{{日本神話}}{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:あめのはつちおのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:天津神]]

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takehazuchi]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takehazuchi]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox deity |image = Bentenjima on Inasa Beach.jpg|right|thumb|250x250px| caption = {{ill|Inasa Beach|ja|稲佐浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inasa Beach|qq=}} ([[:ja:稲佐の浜|稲佐の浜]] ''Inasa-no-hama'') in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|lt=Izumo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}, where the myth places Ōkuninushi's meeting with the messengers of heaven and his surrender to them.|cult_centre={{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} {{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shizumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} {{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}|type=Shinto|name=Takehazuchi|script_name=天羽槌雄神|other_names=Ama no Hazuchi|father={{ill|Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|simple|user:immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}}}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神){{efn|also read as Amenohazuchi}} is the Japanese goddess of weaving<ref name="Nihongi6970">{{cite wikisource|author=Aston|date=1896|Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|chapter=Book II|pages=69–70}}</ref> Also known as Shizuri.<ref name="mizue-2">{{Cite web |last=Mizue |first=Mori |title=Shizuri |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9154 |website={{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}} Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>
She is believed to be a fifth generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} most likely through [[Sukunabikona]] due to the other associations of him.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=天羽槌雄神（あめのはづちのおのかみ） ・建葉槌命（たけはづちのみこと）／日本の神々の話 : 四季歩のつれづれ|url=http://tamtom.blog44.fc2.com/blog-entry-2646.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=tamtom.blog44.fc2.com}}</ref>
She decorated the streamers on the [[Masakaki]] tree used to lure out [[Amaterasu]] from the cave.<ref>{{Cite web|title=神々の庭：天羽槌雄神 (あめのはずちおのかみ)|url=http://shinto.fugarden.com/gosaishin_amenohazutio.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=shinto.fugarden.com}}</ref>
She is a warrior who vanquished the rebellious star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}}.<ref name="宇治谷 1988">{{Harvnb|宇治谷|1988}} 『日本書紀』上 p.64, 58</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /><ref name="mizue-2" />
Appended to the two passages of the subjugation of the earth in the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]] is the mention of a star deity named {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} who resisted till the end, and whom {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} were particularly eager to vanquish. The latter passage states that the being who subdued the star god, referred to as {{Nihongo|''Iwai no nushi''|斎の大人}} is enshrined at Katori, hinting that it might be {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h98EAAAAMAAJ|title=和漢三才図会|author=寺島良安|author2=島田勇雄, 樋口元巳|publisher=平凡社|year=1985|isbn=9784582804478|volume=10|page=71}} subscribes to this view</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /> However, the earlier passage says a god named {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} was the vanquisher of the star god.<ref name="宇治谷 1988" /><ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-03-20|title=Hoshinokamikakaseo {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240320033035/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9400|access-date=2024-03-20|website=web.archive.org}}</ref>
After Ōkuninushi's assent and withdrawal, the two messengers proceeded to destroy everyone and everything who refused to submit to their authority. They then send the god of weaving, Takehazuchi (建葉槌), to subdue the star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|lt=Kagaseo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} (香香背男), the last remaining rebel against Takamagahara (''Nihon Shoki'').<ref name="Nihongi6970" /> After the resistance was gone the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}} could begin.<ref name="mizue-2" />
It is believed it She may either have also been a war goddess based on the "take" in her name analogous to {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} or that She wove the stars into fabric, taming them.<ref name=":0" />
The {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogo Shūi|qq=}} identifies him with Amenohatsuchio the ancestor of the Shizuri clan who did weaving.<ref name="mizue-2" />
At {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|4=fr|5=Shitori-jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} are worshipped as weaving gods<ref name="mizue-2" />.
== Overview ==
Takehazuchi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'' as ''Takehazuchi no Kami''. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', she is referred to as {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shidori deity|ja|倭文神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Shizuri-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. In the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', she is called {{ill|Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} in the Yamashiro Province section and {{ill|Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja|天破命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoha-no-Mikoto|qq=}} in another entry. The ''Anwa Province Imbe Family Genealogy'' (安房国忌部家系), which includes the "Ibe no Sukune Honkeicho" (齋部宿祢本系帳), records her name as {{ill|Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and also lists {{ill|Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武羽槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波豆知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as alternative names.<ref>{{cite book|和書 |title=安房国忌部家系|chapter=齋部宿祢本系帳|section=天羽雷雄命|doi=10.11501/2538213|url={{NDLDC|2538213/55}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|qq=}})</ref>
She is said to have woven sacred cloth (''aya'') to lure [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] out of the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Iwato|qq=}}. The cloth she wove is called {{ill|Shidori cloth|ja|倭文布|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori cloth|qq=}} or simply ''Shidori'' (倭文), a term referring to a type of woven fabric. Other weaving deities include {{ill|Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja|栲幡千々姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, but Takehazuchi no Kami is revered as the '''supreme ancestor of weaving'''. She is also the '''ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''.
She is worshipped as the '''goddess of weaving''' in '''{{ill|Shidori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrines|qq=}}''', '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}}''', '''Hattori Shrines''', and others throughout Japan.
== Genealogy ==
In ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'', she is called the '''far ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''. In ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kujihongi]]'', she is described as the '''distant ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja|倭文造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-no-Mikoto|qq=}}'''.
== Legends and Deeds ==
Takehazuchi no Kami appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' under the name {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. She is said to have '''subjugated the star deity''' '''[[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Amatsu-Mikaboshi]] (also known as Kagaseo no Kami)''', whom '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|qq=}}''' and '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi-no-Kami]]''' were unable to pacify.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=植松安|date=1920-06|title=仮名日本書紀|volume=上巻|pages=87–88|publisher=大同館書店|url={{NDLDC|961006/117}} |doi=10.11501/961006 }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|qq=}})</ref>
==Descendant clans==
{{ill|Nagahata clan|ja|長幡部連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahata clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tanabe clan|ja|田辺宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Takehara clan|ja|竹原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehara clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Okura clan|ja|巨椋連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja|大椋置始連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Okihajime clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Imaki clan|ja|今木連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaki clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tame clan|ja|多米連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tame clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Minu clan|ja|美努連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minu clan|qq=}} ({{ill|Minokuni Rulers|ja|三野県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minokuni Rulers|qq=}}), {{ill|Tottoribe clan|ja|鳥取部連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottoribe clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Jingu clan|ja|神宮部造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Agatanoinukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agatanoinukai clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai clan|qq=}}
== Shrines == {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} is believed to be the ancestor of the {{Nihongo|Shitori clan|倭文氏}} of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}}<ref name="Yoshiki" />.<ref name="mizue-2" />
However, the legends of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} are all centered around {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}}, whom the shrine legend claims arrived in this location from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-taisha|qq=}} by boat. She established her residence on the mountain behind this shrine, and after her death, she was buried in a ''{{ill|kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}}'' constructed in the shrine's precincts.<ref name="Yoshiki">{{cite book|last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese|qq=draft|qq=draft}}</ref> == See Also ==
* [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * ''[[:en:Your Name|Your Name]]'' (2016 anime film), where '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''{{ill|Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|倭文神 建葉槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}'''. * {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:Textile_production_in_Japan|Textile production in Japan]] * [[:en:Shinto_cosmology|Shinto cosmology]] * [[:en:Weaving_in_mythology|Weaving in mythology]]

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takehazuchi]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Takehazuchi]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox deity |image = Bentenjima on Inasa Beach.jpg|right|thumb|250x250px| caption = {{ill|Inasa Beach|ja|稲佐浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inasa Beach|qq=}} ([[:ja:稲佐の浜|稲佐の浜]] ''Inasa-no-hama'') in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|lt=Izumo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}, where the myth places Ōkuninushi's meeting with the messengers of heaven and his surrender to them.|cult_centre={{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} {{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shizumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}} {{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}|type=Shinto|name=Takehazuchi|script_name=天羽槌雄神|other_names=Ama no Hazuchi|father={{ill|Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|simple|user:immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}}}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神){{efn|also read as Amenohazuchi}} is the Japanese goddess of weaving<ref name="Nihongi6970">{{cite wikisource|author=Aston|date=1896|Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|chapter=Book II|pages=69–70}}</ref> Also known as Shizuri.<ref name="mizue-2">{{Cite web |last=Mizue |first=Mori |title=Shizuri |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9154 |website={{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}} Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>
She is believed to be a fifth generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} most likely through [[Sukunabikona]] due to the other associations of him.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=天羽槌雄神（あめのはづちのおのかみ） ・建葉槌命（たけはづちのみこと）／日本の神々の話 : 四季歩のつれづれ|url=http://tamtom.blog44.fc2.com/blog-entry-2646.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=tamtom.blog44.fc2.com}}</ref>
She decorated the streamers on the [[Masakaki]] tree used to lure out [[Amaterasu]] from the cave.<ref>{{Cite web|title=神々の庭：天羽槌雄神 (あめのはずちおのかみ)|url=http://shinto.fugarden.com/gosaishin_amenohazutio.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=shinto.fugarden.com}}</ref>
She is a warrior who vanquished the rebellious star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}}.<ref name="宇治谷 1988">{{Harvnb|宇治谷|1988}} 『日本書紀』上 p.64, 58</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /><ref name="mizue-2" />
Appended to the two passages of the subjugation of the earth in the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]] is the mention of a star deity named {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} who resisted till the end, and whom {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} were particularly eager to vanquish. The latter passage states that the being who subdued the star god, referred to as {{Nihongo|''Iwai no nushi''|斎の大人}} is enshrined at Katori, hinting that it might be {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h98EAAAAMAAJ|title=和漢三才図会|author=寺島良安|author2=島田勇雄, 樋口元巳|publisher=平凡社|year=1985|isbn=9784582804478|volume=10|page=71}} subscribes to this view</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /> However, the earlier passage says a god named {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} was the vanquisher of the star god.<ref name="宇治谷 1988" /><ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-03-20|title=Hoshinokamikakaseo {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240320033035/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9400|access-date=2024-03-20|website=web.archive.org}}</ref>
After Ōkuninushi's assent and withdrawal, the two messengers proceeded to destroy everyone and everything who refused to submit to their authority. They then send the god of weaving, Takehazuchi (建葉槌), to subdue the star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|lt=Kagaseo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} (香香背男), the last remaining rebel against Takamagahara (''Nihon Shoki'').<ref name="Nihongi6970" /> After the resistance was gone the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}} could begin.<ref name="mizue-2" />
It is believed it She may either have also been a war goddess based on the "take" in her name analogous to {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} or that She wove the stars into fabric, taming them.<ref name=":0" />
The {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogo Shūi|qq=}} identifies him with Amenohatsuchio the ancestor of the Shizuri clan who did weaving.<ref name="mizue-2" />
At {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|4=fr|5=Shitori-jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} are worshipped as weaving gods<ref name="mizue-2" />.
== Overview ==
Takehazuchi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'' as ''Takehazuchi no Kami''. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', she is referred to as {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shidori deity|ja|倭文神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Shizuri-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. In the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', she is called {{ill|Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} in the Yamashiro Province section and {{ill|Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja|天破命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoha-no-Mikoto|qq=}} in another entry. The ''Anwa Province Imbe Family Genealogy'' (安房国忌部家系), which includes the "Ibe no Sukune Honkeicho" (齋部宿祢本系帳), records her name as {{ill|Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and also lists {{ill|Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武羽槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波豆知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|qq=}} as alternative names.<ref>{{cite book|和書 |title=安房国忌部家系|chapter=齋部宿祢本系帳|section=天羽雷雄命|doi=10.11501/2538213|url={{NDLDC|2538213/55}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|qq=}})</ref>
She is said to have woven sacred cloth (''aya'') to lure [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] out of the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Iwato|qq=}}. The cloth she wove is called {{ill|Shidori cloth|ja|倭文布|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori cloth|qq=}} or simply ''Shidori'' (倭文), a term referring to a type of woven fabric. Other weaving deities include {{ill|Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja|栲幡千々姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, but Takehazuchi no Kami is revered as the '''supreme ancestor of weaving'''. She is also the '''ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''.
She is worshipped as the '''goddess of weaving''' in '''{{ill|Shidori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrines|qq=}}''', '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}}''', '''Hattori Shrines''', and others throughout Japan.
== Genealogy ==
In ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'', she is called the '''far ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''. In ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kujihongi]]'', she is described as the '''distant ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja|倭文造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-no-Mikoto|qq=}}'''.
== Legends and Deeds ==
Takehazuchi no Kami appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' under the name {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. She is said to have '''subjugated the star deity''' '''[[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Amatsu-Mikaboshi]] (also known as Kagaseo no Kami)''', whom '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|qq=}}''' and '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi-no-Kami]]''' were unable to pacify.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=植松安|date=1920-06|title=仮名日本書紀|volume=上巻|pages=87–88|publisher=大同館書店|url={{NDLDC|961006/117}} |doi=10.11501/961006 }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|qq=}})</ref>
==Descendant clans==
{{ill|Nagahata clan|ja|長幡部連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahata clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tanabe clan|ja|田辺宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Takehara clan|ja|竹原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehara clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Okura clan|ja|巨椋連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja|大椋置始連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Okihajime clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Imaki clan|ja|今木連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaki clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tame clan|ja|多米連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tame clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Minu clan|ja|美努連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minu clan|qq=}} ({{ill|Minokuni Rulers|ja|三野県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minokuni Rulers|qq=}}), {{ill|Tottoribe clan|ja|鳥取部連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottoribe clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Jingu clan|ja|神宮部造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Agatanoinukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agatanoinukai clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai clan|qq=}}
== Shrines == {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} is believed to be the ancestor of the {{Nihongo|Shitori clan|倭文氏}} of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}}<ref name="Yoshiki" />.<ref name="mizue-2" />
However, the legends of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} are all centered around {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}}, whom the shrine legend claims arrived in this location from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-taisha|qq=}} by boat. She established her residence on the mountain behind this shrine, and after her death, she was buried in a ''{{ill|kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}}'' constructed in the shrine's precincts.<ref name="Yoshiki">{{cite book|last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese|qq=draft|qq=draft}}</ref> == See Also ==
* [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * ''[[:en:Your Name|Your Name]]'' (2016 anime film), where '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamizu Shrine|qq=}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''{{ill|Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|倭文神 建葉槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}'''. * {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:Textile_production_in_Japan|Textile production in Japan]] * [[:en:Shinto_cosmology|Shinto cosmology]] * [[:en:Weaving_in_mythology|Weaving in mythology]]

{{Infobox deity
|image = Bentenjima on Inasa Beach.jpg|right|thumb|250x250px|
caption = {{ill|Inasa Beach|ja|稲佐浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inasa Beach|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲佐の浜}} ([[:ja:稲佐の浜|稲佐の浜]] ''Inasa-no-hama'') in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|lt=Izumo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}, where the myth places Ōkuninushi's meeting with the messengers of heaven and his surrender to them.|cult_centre={{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}}
{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}}
{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shizumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}}
{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}|type=Shinto|name=Takehazuchi|script_name=天羽槌雄神|other_names=Ama no Hazuchi|father={{ill|Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}; {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|consort={{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|qid=Q10940674}}; {{ill|Shitateruhime|qid=Q11307233}}|venerated_in={{ill|shitori shrine|qid=Q11387088}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Shitateruhime|qid=Q11307233}}}}
{{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神){{efn|also read as Amenohazuchi}} is the Japanese goddess of weaving<ref name="Nihongi6970">{{cite wikisource|author=Aston|date=1896|Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|chapter=Book II|pages=69–70}}</ref> Also known as Shizuri.<ref name="mizue-2">{{Cite web |last=Mizue |first=Mori |title=Shizuri |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9154 |website={{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}} Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>

She is believed to be a fifth generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} most likely through [[Sukunabikona]] due to the other associations of him.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=天羽槌雄神（あめのはづちのおのかみ）　・建葉槌命（たけはづちのみこと）／日本の神々の話　 : 四季歩のつれづれ|url=http://tamtom.blog44.fc2.com/blog-entry-2646.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=tamtom.blog44.fc2.com}}</ref>

She decorated the streamers on the [[Masakaki]] tree used to lure out [[Amaterasu]] from the cave.<ref>{{Cite web|title=神々の庭：天羽槌雄神 (あめのはずちおのかみ)|url=http://shinto.fugarden.com/gosaishin_amenohazutio.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=shinto.fugarden.com}}</ref> 

She is a warrior who vanquished the rebellious star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}}.<ref name="宇治谷 1988">{{Harvnb|宇治谷|1988}} 『日本書紀』上 p.64, 58</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /><ref name="mizue-2" />

Appended to the two passages of the subjugation of the earth in the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]] is the mention of a star deity named {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} who resisted till the end, and whom {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} were particularly eager to vanquish. The latter passage states that the being who subdued the star god, referred to as {{Nihongo|''Iwai no nushi''|斎の大人}} is enshrined at Katori, hinting that it might be {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h98EAAAAMAAJ|title=和漢三才図会|author=寺島良安|author2=島田勇雄, 樋口元巳|publisher=平凡社|year=1985|isbn=9784582804478|volume=10|page=71}} subscribes to this view</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /> However, the earlier passage says a god named {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} was the vanquisher of the star god.<ref name="宇治谷 1988" /><ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-03-20|title=Hoshinokamikakaseo {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240320033035/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9400|access-date=2024-03-20|website=web.archive.org}}</ref>

After Ōkuninushi's assent and withdrawal, the two messengers proceeded to destroy everyone and everything who refused to submit to their authority. They then send the god of weaving, Takehazuchi (建葉槌), to subdue the star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|lt=Kagaseo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} (香香背男), the last remaining rebel against Takamagahara (''Nihon Shoki'').<ref name="Nihongi6970" /> After the resistance was gone the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}} could begin.<ref name="mizue-2" />

It is believed it She may either have also been a war goddess based on the "take" in her name analogous to {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} or that She wove the stars into fabric, taming them.<ref name=":0" />

The {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogo Shūi|qq=}} identifies him with Amenohatsuchio the ancestor of the Shizuri clan who did weaving.<ref name="mizue-2" />

At {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|4=fr|5=Shitori-jinja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} are worshipped as weaving gods<ref name="mizue-2" />. 

== Overview ==

Takehazuchi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'' as ''Takehazuchi no Kami''. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', she is referred to as {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shidori deity|ja|倭文神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Shizuri-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. In the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', she is called {{ill|Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Yamashiro Province section and {{ill|Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja|天破命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoha-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in another entry. The ''Anwa Province Imbe Family Genealogy'' (安房国忌部家系), which includes the "Ibe no Sukune Honkeicho" (齋部宿祢本系帳), records her name as {{ill|Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷雄命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940642}} and also lists {{ill|Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武羽槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波豆知命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as alternative names.<ref>{{cite book|和書 |title=安房国忌部家系|chapter=齋部宿祢本系帳|section=天羽雷雄命|doi=10.11501/2538213|url={{NDLDC|2538213/55}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>

She is said to have woven sacred cloth (''aya'') to lure [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] out of the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Iwato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3231835}}. The cloth she wove is called {{ill|Shidori cloth|ja|倭文布|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori cloth|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or simply ''Shidori'' (倭文), a term referring to a type of woven fabric. Other weaving deities include {{ill|Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja|栲幡千々姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}} and {{ill|Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, but Takehazuchi no Kami is revered as the '''supreme ancestor of weaving'''. She is also the '''ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''.

She is worshipped as the '''goddess of weaving''' in '''{{ill|Shidori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrines|qq=|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}''', '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}}''', '''Hattori Shrines''', and others throughout Japan.

== Genealogy ==

In ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'', she is called the '''far ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''. In ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kujihongi]]'', she is described as the '''distant ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja|倭文造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.

== Legends and Deeds ==

Takehazuchi no Kami appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' under the name {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. She is said to have '''subjugated the star deity''' '''[[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Amatsu-Mikaboshi]] (also known as Kagaseo no Kami)''', whom '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|武甕槌命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1502690}}''' and '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi-no-Kami]]''' were unable to pacify.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=植松安|date=1920-06|title=仮名日本書紀|volume=上巻|pages=87–88|publisher=大同館書店|url={{NDLDC|961006/117}} |doi=10.11501/961006 }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>

==Descendant clans==

{{ill|Nagahata clan|ja|長幡部連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahata clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tanabe clan|ja|田辺宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takehara clan|ja|竹原氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Okura clan|ja|巨椋連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja|大椋置始連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Okihajime clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Imaki clan|ja|今木連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tame clan|ja|多米連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tame clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Minu clan|ja|美努連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minu clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Minokuni Rulers|ja|三野県主|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minokuni Rulers|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Tottoribe clan|ja|鳥取部連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottoribe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Jingu clan|ja|神宮部造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Agatanoinukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agatanoinukai clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai clan|qq=}}

== Shrines ==
{{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} is believed to be the ancestor of the {{Nihongo|Shitori clan|倭文氏}} of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}}<ref name="Yoshiki" />.<ref name="mizue-2" /> 

However, the legends of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} are all centered around {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}}, whom the shrine legend claims arrived in this location from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-taisha|qq=}} by boat. She established her residence on the mountain behind this shrine, and after her death, she was buried in a ''{{ill|kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}}'' constructed in the shrine's precincts.<ref name="Yoshiki">{{cite book|last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese|qq=draft|qq=draft}}</ref>
