{{Infobox deity
|image = Bentenjima on Inasa Beach.jpg|right|thumb|250x250px|
caption = {{ill|Inasa Beach|ja|稲佐浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inasa Beach|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲佐の浜}} ([[:ja:稲佐の浜|稲佐の浜]] ''Inasa-no-hama'') in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|lt=Izumo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}, where the myth places Ōkuninushi's meeting with the messengers of heaven and his surrender to them.|cult_centre={{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}}
{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}}
{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shizumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}}
{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}|type=Shinto|name=Takehazuchi|script_name=天羽槌雄神|other_names=Ama no Hazuchi|father={{ill|Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}; {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|consort={{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|WD=Q10940674}}; {{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}|venerated_in={{ill|shitori shrine|WD=Q11387088}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}}}
{{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神){{efn|also read as Amenohazuchi}} is the Japanese goddess of weaving<ref name="Nihongi6970">{{cite wikisource|author=Aston|date=1896|Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|chapter=Book II|pages=69–70}}</ref> Also known as Shizuri.<ref name="mizue-2">{{Cite web |last=Mizue |first=Mori |title=Shizuri |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9154 |website={{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}} Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>

She is believed to be a fifth generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} most likely through [[Sukunabikona]] due to the other associations of him.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=天羽槌雄神（あめのはづちのおのかみ）　・建葉槌命（たけはづちのみこと）／日本の神々の話　 : 四季歩のつれづれ|url=http://tamtom.blog44.fc2.com/blog-entry-2646.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=tamtom.blog44.fc2.com}}</ref>

She decorated the streamers on the [[Masakaki]] tree used to lure out [[Amaterasu]] from the cave.<ref>{{Cite web|title=神々の庭：天羽槌雄神 (あめのはずちおのかみ)|url=http://shinto.fugarden.com/gosaishin_amenohazutio.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=shinto.fugarden.com}}</ref> 

She is a warrior who vanquished the rebellious star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}}.<ref name="宇治谷 1988">{{Harvnb|宇治谷|1988}} 『日本書紀』上 p.64, 58</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /><ref name="mizue-2" />

Appended to the two passages of the subjugation of the earth in the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]] is the mention of a star deity named {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} who resisted till the end, and whom {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} were particularly eager to vanquish. The latter passage states that the being who subdued the star god, referred to as {{Nihongo|''Iwai no nushi''|斎の大人}} is enshrined at Katori, hinting that it might be {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h98EAAAAMAAJ|title=和漢三才図会|author=寺島良安|author2=島田勇雄, 樋口元巳|publisher=平凡社|year=1985|isbn=9784582804478|volume=10|page=71}} subscribes to this view</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /> However, the earlier passage says a god named {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} was the vanquisher of the star god.<ref name="宇治谷 1988" /><ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-03-20|title=Hoshinokamikakaseo {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240320033035/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9400|access-date=2024-03-20|website=web.archive.org}}</ref>

After Ōkuninushi's assent and withdrawal, the two messengers proceeded to destroy everyone and everything who refused to submit to their authority. They then send the god of weaving, Takehazuchi (建葉槌), to subdue the star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|lt=Kagaseo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} (香香背男), the last remaining rebel against Takamagahara (''Nihon Shoki'').<ref name="Nihongi6970" /> After the resistance was gone the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}} could begin.<ref name="mizue-2" />

It is believed it She may either have also been a war goddess based on the "take" in her name analogous to {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} or that She wove the stars into fabric, taming them.<ref name=":0" />

The {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogo Shūi|qq=}} identifies him with Amenohatsuchio the ancestor of the Shizuri clan who did weaving.<ref name="mizue-2" />

At {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|4=fr|5=Shitori-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} are worshipped as weaving gods<ref name="mizue-2" />. 

== Overview ==

Takehazuchi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'' as ''Takehazuchi no Kami''. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', she is referred to as {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shidori deity|ja|倭文神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Shizuri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. In the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', she is called {{ill|Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Yamashiro Province section and {{ill|Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja|天破命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in another entry. The ''Anwa Province Imbe Family Genealogy'' (安房国忌部家系), which includes the "Ibe no Sukune Honkeicho" (齋部宿祢本系帳), records her name as {{ill|Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940642}} and also lists {{ill|Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武羽槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波豆知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as alternative names.<ref>{{cite book|和書 |title=安房国忌部家系|chapter=齋部宿祢本系帳|section=天羽雷雄命|doi=10.11501/2538213|url={{NDLDC|2538213/55}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>

She is said to have woven sacred cloth (''aya'') to lure [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] out of the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Iwato|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3231835}}. The cloth she wove is called {{ill|Shidori cloth|ja|倭文布|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori cloth|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or simply ''Shidori'' (倭文), a term referring to a type of woven fabric. Other weaving deities include {{ill|Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja|栲幡千々姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}} and {{ill|Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, but Takehazuchi no Kami is revered as the '''supreme ancestor of weaving'''. She is also the '''ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''.

She is worshipped as the '''goddess of weaving''' in '''{{ill|Shidori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrines|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}''', '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}}''', '''Hattori Shrines''', and others throughout Japan.

== Genealogy ==

In ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'', she is called the '''far ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''. In ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kujihongi]]'', she is described as the '''distant ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja|倭文造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.

== Legends and Deeds ==

Takehazuchi no Kami appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' under the name {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. She is said to have '''subjugated the star deity''' '''[[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Amatsu-Mikaboshi]] (also known as Kagaseo no Kami)''', whom '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1502690}}''' and '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi-no-Kami]]''' were unable to pacify.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=植松安|date=1920-06|title=仮名日本書紀|volume=上巻|pages=87–88|publisher=大同館書店|url={{NDLDC|961006/117}} |doi=10.11501/961006 }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>

==Descendant clans==

{{ill|Nagahata clan|ja|長幡部連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahata clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tanabe clan|ja|田辺宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takehara clan|ja|竹原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Okura clan|ja|巨椋連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja|大椋置始連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Imaki clan|ja|今木連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tame clan|ja|多米連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tame clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Minu clan|ja|美努連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Minokuni Rulers|ja|三野県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minokuni Rulers|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Tottoribe clan|ja|鳥取部連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottoribe clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Jingu clan|ja|神宮部造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Agatanoinukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agatanoinukai clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai clan|qq=}}

== Shrines ==
{{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} is believed to be the ancestor of the {{Nihongo|Shitori clan|倭文氏}} of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}}<ref name="Yoshiki" />.<ref name="mizue-2" /> 

However, the legends of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} are all centered around {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}}, whom the shrine legend claims arrived in this location from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-taisha|qq=}} by boat. She established her residence on the mountain behind this shrine, and after her death, she was buried in a ''{{ill|kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}}'' constructed in the shrine's precincts.<ref name="Yoshiki">{{cite book|last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese|qq=draft}}</ref>

{{Infobox deity
|image = Bentenjima on Inasa Beach.jpg|right|thumb|250x250px|
caption = {{ill|Inasa Beach|ja|稲佐浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inasa Beach|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲佐の浜}} ([[:ja:稲佐の浜|稲佐の浜]] ''Inasa-no-hama'') in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|lt=Izumo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}, where the myth places Ōkuninushi's meeting with the messengers of heaven and his surrender to them.|cult_centre={{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}}
{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}}
{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shizumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}}
{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}|type=Shinto|name=Takehazuchi|script_name=天羽槌雄神|other_names=Ama no Hazuchi|father={{ill|Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}; {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|consort={{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|WD=Q10940674}}; {{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}|venerated_in={{ill|shitori shrine|WD=Q11387088}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}}}
{{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神){{efn|also read as Amenohazuchi}} is the Japanese goddess of weaving<ref name="Nihongi6970">{{cite wikisource|author=Aston|date=1896|Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|chapter=Book II|pages=69–70}}</ref> Also known as Shizuri.<ref name="mizue-2">{{Cite web |last=Mizue |first=Mori |title=Shizuri |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9154 |website={{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}} Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>

She is believed to be a fifth generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} most likely through [[Sukunabikona]] due to the other associations of him.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=天羽槌雄神（あめのはづちのおのかみ）　・建葉槌命（たけはづちのみこと）／日本の神々の話　 : 四季歩のつれづれ|url=http://tamtom.blog44.fc2.com/blog-entry-2646.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=tamtom.blog44.fc2.com}}</ref>

She decorated the streamers on the [[Masakaki]] tree used to lure out [[Amaterasu]] from the cave.<ref>{{Cite web|title=神々の庭：天羽槌雄神 (あめのはずちおのかみ)|url=http://shinto.fugarden.com/gosaishin_amenohazutio.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=shinto.fugarden.com}}</ref> 

She is a warrior who vanquished the rebellious star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}}.<ref name="宇治谷 1988">{{Harvnb|宇治谷|1988}} 『日本書紀』上 p.64, 58</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /><ref name="mizue-2" />

Appended to the two passages of the subjugation of the earth in the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]] is the mention of a star deity named {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} who resisted till the end, and whom {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} were particularly eager to vanquish. The latter passage states that the being who subdued the star god, referred to as {{Nihongo|''Iwai no nushi''|斎の大人}} is enshrined at Katori, hinting that it might be {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h98EAAAAMAAJ|title=和漢三才図会|author=寺島良安|author2=島田勇雄, 樋口元巳|publisher=平凡社|year=1985|isbn=9784582804478|volume=10|page=71}} subscribes to this view</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /> However, the earlier passage says a god named {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} was the vanquisher of the star god.<ref name="宇治谷 1988" /><ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-03-20|title=Hoshinokamikakaseo {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240320033035/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9400|access-date=2024-03-20|website=web.archive.org}}</ref>

After Ōkuninushi's assent and withdrawal, the two messengers proceeded to destroy everyone and everything who refused to submit to their authority. They then send the god of weaving, Takehazuchi (建葉槌), to subdue the star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|lt=Kagaseo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} (香香背男), the last remaining rebel against Takamagahara (''Nihon Shoki'').<ref name="Nihongi6970" /> After the resistance was gone the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}} could begin.<ref name="mizue-2" />

It is believed it She may either have also been a war goddess based on the "take" in her name analogous to {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} or that She wove the stars into fabric, taming them.<ref name=":0" />

The {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogo Shūi|qq=}} identifies him with Amenohatsuchio the ancestor of the Shizuri clan who did weaving.<ref name="mizue-2" />

At {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|4=fr|5=Shitori-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} are worshipped as weaving gods<ref name="mizue-2" />. 

== Overview ==

Takehazuchi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'' as ''Takehazuchi no Kami''. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', she is referred to as {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shidori deity|ja|倭文神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Shizuri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. In the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', she is called {{ill|Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Yamashiro Province section and {{ill|Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja|天破命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in another entry. The ''Anwa Province Imbe Family Genealogy'' (安房国忌部家系), which includes the "Ibe no Sukune Honkeicho" (齋部宿祢本系帳), records her name as {{ill|Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940642}} and also lists {{ill|Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武羽槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波豆知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as alternative names.<ref>{{cite book|和書 |title=安房国忌部家系|chapter=齋部宿祢本系帳|section=天羽雷雄命|doi=10.11501/2538213|url={{NDLDC|2538213/55}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>

She is said to have woven sacred cloth (''aya'') to lure [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] out of the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Iwato|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3231835}}. The cloth she wove is called {{ill|Shidori cloth|ja|倭文布|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori cloth|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or simply ''Shidori'' (倭文), a term referring to a type of woven fabric. Other weaving deities include {{ill|Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja|栲幡千々姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}} and {{ill|Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, but Takehazuchi no Kami is revered as the '''supreme ancestor of weaving'''. She is also the '''ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''.

She is worshipped as the '''goddess of weaving''' in '''{{ill|Shidori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrines|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}''', '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}}''', '''Hattori Shrines''', and others throughout Japan.

== Genealogy ==

In ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'', she is called the '''far ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''. In ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kujihongi]]'', she is described as the '''distant ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja|倭文造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.

== Legends and Deeds ==

Takehazuchi no Kami appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' under the name {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. She is said to have '''subjugated the star deity''' '''[[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Amatsu-Mikaboshi]] (also known as Kagaseo no Kami)''', whom '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1502690}}''' and '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi-no-Kami]]''' were unable to pacify.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=植松安|date=1920-06|title=仮名日本書紀|volume=上巻|pages=87–88|publisher=大同館書店|url={{NDLDC|961006/117}} |doi=10.11501/961006 }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>

==Descendant clans==

{{ill|Nagahata clan|ja|長幡部連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahata clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tanabe clan|ja|田辺宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takehara clan|ja|竹原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Okura clan|ja|巨椋連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja|大椋置始連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Imaki clan|ja|今木連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tame clan|ja|多米連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tame clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Minu clan|ja|美努連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Minokuni Rulers|ja|三野県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minokuni Rulers|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Tottoribe clan|ja|鳥取部連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottoribe clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Jingu clan|ja|神宮部造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Agatanoinukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agatanoinukai clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai clan|qq=}}

== Shrines ==
{{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} is believed to be the ancestor of the {{Nihongo|Shitori clan|倭文氏}} of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}}<ref name="Yoshiki" />.<ref name="mizue-2" /> 

However, the legends of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} are all centered around {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}}, whom the shrine legend claims arrived in this location from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-taisha|qq=}} by boat. She established her residence on the mountain behind this shrine, and after her death, she was buried in a ''{{ill|kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}}'' constructed in the shrine's precincts.<ref name="Yoshiki">{{cite book|last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese|qq=draft}}</ref>

{{Infobox deity
|image = Bentenjima on Inasa Beach.jpg|right|thumb|250x250px|
caption = {{ill|Inasa Beach|ja|稲佐浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inasa Beach|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲佐の浜}} ([[:ja:稲佐の浜|稲佐の浜]] ''Inasa-no-hama'') in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|lt=Izumo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}, where the myth places Ōkuninushi's meeting with the messengers of heaven and his surrender to them.|cult_centre={{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}}
{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}}
{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shizumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}}
{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}|type=Shinto|name=Takehazuchi|script_name=天羽槌雄神|other_names=Ama no Hazuchi|father={{ill|Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}; {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|consort={{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|WD=Q10940674}}; {{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}|venerated_in={{ill|shitori shrine|WD=Q11387088}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}}}
{{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神){{efn|also read as Amenohazuchi}} is the Japanese goddess of weaving<ref name="Nihongi6970">{{cite wikisource|author=Aston|date=1896|Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|chapter=Book II|pages=69–70}}</ref> Also known as Shizuri.<ref name="mizue-2">{{Cite web |last=Mizue |first=Mori |title=Shizuri |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9154 |website={{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}} Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>

She is believed to be a fifth generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} most likely through [[Sukunabikona]] due to the other associations of him.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=天羽槌雄神（あめのはづちのおのかみ）　・建葉槌命（たけはづちのみこと）／日本の神々の話　 : 四季歩のつれづれ|url=http://tamtom.blog44.fc2.com/blog-entry-2646.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=tamtom.blog44.fc2.com}}</ref>

She decorated the streamers on the [[Masakaki]] tree used to lure out [[Amaterasu]] from the cave.<ref>{{Cite web|title=神々の庭：天羽槌雄神 (あめのはずちおのかみ)|url=http://shinto.fugarden.com/gosaishin_amenohazutio.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=shinto.fugarden.com}}</ref> 

She is a warrior who vanquished the rebellious star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}}.<ref name="宇治谷 1988">{{Harvnb|宇治谷|1988}} 『日本書紀』上 p.64, 58</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /><ref name="mizue-2" />

Appended to the two passages of the subjugation of the earth in the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]] is the mention of a star deity named {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} who resisted till the end, and whom {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} were particularly eager to vanquish. The latter passage states that the being who subdued the star god, referred to as {{Nihongo|''Iwai no nushi''|斎の大人}} is enshrined at Katori, hinting that it might be {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h98EAAAAMAAJ|title=和漢三才図会|author=寺島良安|author2=島田勇雄, 樋口元巳|publisher=平凡社|year=1985|isbn=9784582804478|volume=10|page=71}} subscribes to this view</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /> However, the earlier passage says a god named {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} was the vanquisher of the star god.<ref name="宇治谷 1988" /><ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-03-20|title=Hoshinokamikakaseo {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240320033035/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9400|access-date=2024-03-20|website=web.archive.org}}</ref>

After Ōkuninushi's assent and withdrawal, the two messengers proceeded to destroy everyone and everything who refused to submit to their authority. They then send the god of weaving, Takehazuchi (建葉槌), to subdue the star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|lt=Kagaseo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} (香香背男), the last remaining rebel against Takamagahara (''Nihon Shoki'').<ref name="Nihongi6970" /> After the resistance was gone the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}} could begin.<ref name="mizue-2" />

It is believed it She may either have also been a war goddess based on the "take" in her name analogous to {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} or that She wove the stars into fabric, taming them.<ref name=":0" />

The {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogo Shūi|qq=}} identifies him with Amenohatsuchio the ancestor of the Shizuri clan who did weaving.<ref name="mizue-2" />

At {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|4=fr|5=Shitori-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} are worshipped as weaving gods<ref name="mizue-2" />. 

== Overview ==

Takehazuchi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'' as ''Takehazuchi no Kami''. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', she is referred to as {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shidori deity|ja|倭文神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Shizuri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. In the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', she is called {{ill|Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Yamashiro Province section and {{ill|Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja|天破命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in another entry. The ''Anwa Province Imbe Family Genealogy'' (安房国忌部家系), which includes the "Ibe no Sukune Honkeicho" (齋部宿祢本系帳), records her name as {{ill|Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940642}} and also lists {{ill|Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武羽槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波豆知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as alternative names.<ref>{{cite book|和書 |title=安房国忌部家系|chapter=齋部宿祢本系帳|section=天羽雷雄命|doi=10.11501/2538213|url={{NDLDC|2538213/55}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>

She is said to have woven sacred cloth (''aya'') to lure [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] out of the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Iwato|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3231835}}. The cloth she wove is called {{ill|Shidori cloth|ja|倭文布|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori cloth|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or simply ''Shidori'' (倭文), a term referring to a type of woven fabric. Other weaving deities include {{ill|Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja|栲幡千々姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}} and {{ill|Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, but Takehazuchi no Kami is revered as the '''supreme ancestor of weaving'''. She is also the '''ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''.

She is worshipped as the '''goddess of weaving''' in '''{{ill|Shidori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrines|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}''', '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}}''', '''Hattori Shrines''', and others throughout Japan.

== Genealogy ==

In ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'', she is called the '''far ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''. In ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kujihongi]]'', she is described as the '''distant ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja|倭文造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.

== Legends and Deeds ==

Takehazuchi no Kami appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' under the name {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. She is said to have '''subjugated the star deity''' '''[[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Amatsu-Mikaboshi]] (also known as Kagaseo no Kami)''', whom '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1502690}}''' and '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi-no-Kami]]''' were unable to pacify.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=植松安|date=1920-06|title=仮名日本書紀|volume=上巻|pages=87–88|publisher=大同館書店|url={{NDLDC|961006/117}} |doi=10.11501/961006 }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>

==Descendant clans==

{{ill|Nagahata clan|ja|長幡部連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahata clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tanabe clan|ja|田辺宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takehara clan|ja|竹原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Okura clan|ja|巨椋連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja|大椋置始連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Imaki clan|ja|今木連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tame clan|ja|多米連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tame clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Minu clan|ja|美努連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Minokuni Rulers|ja|三野県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minokuni Rulers|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Tottoribe clan|ja|鳥取部連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottoribe clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Jingu clan|ja|神宮部造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Agatanoinukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agatanoinukai clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai clan|qq=}}

== Shrines ==
{{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} is believed to be the ancestor of the {{Nihongo|Shitori clan|倭文氏}} of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}}<ref name="Yoshiki" />.<ref name="mizue-2" /> 

However, the legends of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} are all centered around {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}}, whom the shrine legend claims arrived in this location from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-taisha|qq=}} by boat. She established her residence on the mountain behind this shrine, and after her death, she was buried in a ''{{ill|kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}}'' constructed in the shrine's precincts.<ref name="Yoshiki">{{cite book|last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese|qq=draft}}</ref>

{{Infobox deity
|image = Bentenjima on Inasa Beach.jpg|right|thumb|250x250px|
caption = {{ill|Inasa Beach|ja|稲佐浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inasa Beach|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲佐の浜}} ([[:ja:稲佐の浜|稲佐の浜]] ''Inasa-no-hama'') in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|lt=Izumo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}, where the myth places Ōkuninushi's meeting with the messengers of heaven and his surrender to them.|cult_centre={{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}}
{{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}}
{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shizumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}}
{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}|type=Shinto|name=Takehazuchi|script_name=天羽槌雄神|other_names=Ama no Hazuchi|father={{ill|Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}; {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|consort={{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|WD=Q10940674}}; {{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}|venerated_in={{ill|shitori shrine|WD=Q11387088}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}}}
{{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神){{efn|also read as Amenohazuchi}} is the Japanese goddess of weaving<ref name="Nihongi6970">{{cite wikisource|author=Aston|date=1896|Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|chapter=Book II|pages=69–70}}</ref> Also known as Shizuri.<ref name="mizue-2">{{Cite web |last=Mizue |first=Mori |title=Shizuri |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9154 |website={{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}} Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>

She is believed to be a fifth generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} most likely through [[Sukunabikona]] due to the other associations of him.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=天羽槌雄神（あめのはづちのおのかみ）　・建葉槌命（たけはづちのみこと）／日本の神々の話　 : 四季歩のつれづれ|url=http://tamtom.blog44.fc2.com/blog-entry-2646.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=tamtom.blog44.fc2.com}}</ref>

She decorated the streamers on the [[Masakaki]] tree used to lure out [[Amaterasu]] from the cave.<ref>{{Cite web|title=神々の庭：天羽槌雄神 (あめのはずちおのかみ)|url=http://shinto.fugarden.com/gosaishin_amenohazutio.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=shinto.fugarden.com}}</ref> 

She is a warrior who vanquished the rebellious star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}}.<ref name="宇治谷 1988">{{Harvnb|宇治谷|1988}} 『日本書紀』上 p.64, 58</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /><ref name="mizue-2" />

Appended to the two passages of the subjugation of the earth in the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]] is the mention of a star deity named {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} who resisted till the end, and whom {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} were particularly eager to vanquish. The latter passage states that the being who subdued the star god, referred to as {{Nihongo|''Iwai no nushi''|斎の大人}} is enshrined at Katori, hinting that it might be {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h98EAAAAMAAJ|title=和漢三才図会|author=寺島良安|author2=島田勇雄, 樋口元巳|publisher=平凡社|year=1985|isbn=9784582804478|volume=10|page=71}} subscribes to this view</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /> However, the earlier passage says a god named {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} was the vanquisher of the star god.<ref name="宇治谷 1988" /><ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-03-20|title=Hoshinokamikakaseo {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240320033035/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9400|access-date=2024-03-20|website=web.archive.org}}</ref>

After Ōkuninushi's assent and withdrawal, the two messengers proceeded to destroy everyone and everything who refused to submit to their authority. They then send the god of weaving, Takehazuchi (建葉槌), to subdue the star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|lt=Kagaseo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} (香香背男), the last remaining rebel against Takamagahara (''Nihon Shoki'').<ref name="Nihongi6970" /> After the resistance was gone the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}} could begin.<ref name="mizue-2" />

It is believed it She may either have also been a war goddess based on the "take" in her name analogous to {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} or that She wove the stars into fabric, taming them.<ref name=":0" />

The {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogo Shūi|qq=}} identifies him with Amenohatsuchio the ancestor of the Shizuri clan who did weaving.<ref name="mizue-2" />

At {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|4=fr|5=Shitori-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} are worshipped as weaving gods<ref name="mizue-2" />. 

== Overview ==

Takehazuchi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'' as ''Takehazuchi no Kami''. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', she is referred to as {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shidori deity|ja|倭文神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Shizuri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. In the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', she is called {{ill|Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Yamashiro Province section and {{ill|Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja|天破命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in another entry. The ''Anwa Province Imbe Family Genealogy'' (安房国忌部家系), which includes the "Ibe no Sukune Honkeicho" (齋部宿祢本系帳), records her name as {{ill|Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940642}} and also lists {{ill|Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武羽槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波豆知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as alternative names.<ref>{{cite book|和書 |title=安房国忌部家系|chapter=齋部宿祢本系帳|section=天羽雷雄命|doi=10.11501/2538213|url={{NDLDC|2538213/55}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>

She is said to have woven sacred cloth (''aya'') to lure [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] out of the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Iwato|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3231835}}. The cloth she wove is called {{ill|Shidori cloth|ja|倭文布|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori cloth|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or simply ''Shidori'' (倭文), a term referring to a type of woven fabric. Other weaving deities include {{ill|Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja|栲幡千々姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}} and {{ill|Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, but Takehazuchi no Kami is revered as the '''supreme ancestor of weaving'''. She is also the '''ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''.

She is worshipped as the '''goddess of weaving''' in '''{{ill|Shidori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrines|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}''', '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}}''', '''Hattori Shrines''', and others throughout Japan.

== Genealogy ==

In ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'', she is called the '''far ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''. In ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kujihongi]]'', she is described as the '''distant ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja|倭文造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.

== Legends and Deeds ==

Takehazuchi no Kami appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' under the name {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. She is said to have '''subjugated the star deity''' '''[[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Amatsu-Mikaboshi]] (also known as Kagaseo no Kami)''', whom '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1502690}}''' and '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi-no-Kami]]''' were unable to pacify.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=植松安|date=1920-06|title=仮名日本書紀|volume=上巻|pages=87–88|publisher=大同館書店|url={{NDLDC|961006/117}} |doi=10.11501/961006 }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>

==Descendant clans==

{{ill|Nagahata clan|ja|長幡部連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahata clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tanabe clan|ja|田辺宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takehara clan|ja|竹原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Okura clan|ja|巨椋連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja|大椋置始連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Imaki clan|ja|今木連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tame clan|ja|多米連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tame clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Minu clan|ja|美努連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Minokuni Rulers|ja|三野県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minokuni Rulers|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Tottoribe clan|ja|鳥取部連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottoribe clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Jingu clan|ja|神宮部造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Agatanoinukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agatanoinukai clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai clan|qq=}}

== Shrines ==
{{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} is believed to be the ancestor of the {{Nihongo|Shitori clan|倭文氏}} of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}}<ref name="Yoshiki" />.<ref name="mizue-2" /> 

However, the legends of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} are all centered around {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}}, whom the shrine legend claims arrived in this location from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-taisha|qq=}} by boat. She established her residence on the mountain behind this shrine, and after her death, she was buried in a ''{{ill|kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}}'' constructed in the shrine's precincts.<ref name="Yoshiki">{{cite book|last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese|qq=draft}}</ref>

{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shizumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}}
{{ill|Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Shitori niimasu Amenohaikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|qq=}}|type=Shinto|name=Takehazuchi|script_name=天羽槌雄神|other_names=Ama no Hazuchi|father={{ill|Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|ja|天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|simple|User:Immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanohiwashi no Mikoto|qq=}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}; {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|consort={{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|WD=Q10940674}}; {{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}|venerated_in={{ill|shitori shrine|WD=Q11387088}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Shitateruhime|WD=Q11307233}}}}
{{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (天羽槌雄神){{efn|also read as Amenohazuchi}} is the Japanese goddess of weaving<ref name="Nihongi6970">{{cite wikisource|author=Aston|date=1896|Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|chapter=Book II|pages=69–70}}</ref> Also known as Shizuri.<ref name="mizue-2">{{Cite web |last=Mizue |first=Mori |title=Shizuri |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9154 |website={{ill|Kokugakuin University|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokugakuin University|qq=}} Encyclopedia of Shinto}}</ref>

She is believed to be a fifth generation descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} most likely through [[Sukunabikona]] due to the other associations of him.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=天羽槌雄神（あめのはづちのおのかみ）　・建葉槌命（たけはづちのみこと）／日本の神々の話　 : 四季歩のつれづれ|url=http://tamtom.blog44.fc2.com/blog-entry-2646.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=tamtom.blog44.fc2.com}}</ref>

She decorated the streamers on the [[Masakaki]] tree used to lure out [[Amaterasu]] from the cave.<ref>{{Cite web|title=神々の庭：天羽槌雄神 (あめのはずちおのかみ)|url=http://shinto.fugarden.com/gosaishin_amenohazutio.html|access-date=2024-05-23|website=shinto.fugarden.com}}</ref> 

She is a warrior who vanquished the rebellious star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} during the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}}.<ref name="宇治谷 1988">{{Harvnb|宇治谷|1988}} 『日本書紀』上 p.64, 58</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /><ref name="mizue-2" />

Appended to the two passages of the subjugation of the earth in the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]] is the mention of a star deity named {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} who resisted till the end, and whom {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} were particularly eager to vanquish. The latter passage states that the being who subdued the star god, referred to as {{Nihongo|''Iwai no nushi''|斎の大人}} is enshrined at Katori, hinting that it might be {{ill|Futsunushi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h98EAAAAMAAJ|title=和漢三才図会|author=寺島良安|author2=島田勇雄, 樋口元巳|publisher=平凡社|year=1985|isbn=9784582804478|volume=10|page=71}} subscribes to this view</ref><ref name="Nihongi6970" /> However, the earlier passage says a god named {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} was the vanquisher of the star god.<ref name="宇治谷 1988" /><ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-03-20|title=Hoshinokamikakaseo {{!}} 國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240320033035/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9400|access-date=2024-03-20|website=web.archive.org}}</ref>

After Ōkuninushi's assent and withdrawal, the two messengers proceeded to destroy everyone and everything who refused to submit to their authority. They then send the god of weaving, Takehazuchi (建葉槌), to subdue the star god {{ill|Amatsu-Mikaboshi|lt=Kagaseo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsu-Mikaboshi|qq=}} (香香背男), the last remaining rebel against Takamagahara (''Nihon Shoki'').<ref name="Nihongi6970" /> After the resistance was gone the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson kōrin|qq=}} could begin.<ref name="mizue-2" />

It is believed it She may either have also been a war goddess based on the "take" in her name analogous to {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}} or that She wove the stars into fabric, taming them.<ref name=":0" />

The {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogo Shūi|qq=}} identifies him with Amenohatsuchio the ancestor of the Shizuri clan who did weaving.<ref name="mizue-2" />

At {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|4=fr|5=Shitori-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} {{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} are worshipped as weaving gods<ref name="mizue-2" />. 

== Overview ==

Takehazuchi no Kami is mentioned in the ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'' as ''Takehazuchi no Kami''. In the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'', she is referred to as {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Shidori deity|ja|倭文神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|simple|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Shizuri-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. In the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', she is called {{ill|Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohachi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the Yamashiro Province section and {{ill|Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja|天破命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoha-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in another entry. The ''Anwa Province Imbe Family Genealogy'' (安房国忌部家系), which includes the "Ibe no Sukune Honkeicho" (齋部宿祢本系帳), records her name as {{ill|Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽雷雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Haikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940642}} and also lists {{ill|Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|武羽槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja|止与波豆知命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohazuji-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as alternative names.<ref>{{cite book|和書 |title=安房国忌部家系|chapter=齋部宿祢本系帳|section=天羽雷雄命|doi=10.11501/2538213|url={{NDLDC|2538213/55}} }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>

She is said to have woven sacred cloth (''aya'') to lure [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] out of the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Iwato|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3231835}}. The cloth she wove is called {{ill|Shidori cloth|ja|倭文布|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori cloth|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or simply ''Shidori'' (倭文), a term referring to a type of woven fabric. Other weaving deities include {{ill|Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja|栲幡千々姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuhatachichihime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}} and {{ill|Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|lt=Ame-no-Tanabatahime|1=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=天棚機姫神|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|4=de|5=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|6=fr|7=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|8=zh|9=天棚機姫神|10=nl|11=Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotatakahime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, but Takehazuchi no Kami is revered as the '''supreme ancestor of weaving'''. She is also the '''ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''.

She is worshipped as the '''goddess of weaving''' in '''{{ill|Shidori Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori Shrines|1=Shizuri Shrine (worship)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387088}}''', '''{{ill|Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizu Shrine|qq=}}''', '''Hattori Shrines''', and others throughout Japan.

== Genealogy ==

In ''[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]'', she is called the '''far ancestor of the {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}'''. In ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kujihongi]]'', she is described as the '''distant ancestor of the {{ill|Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja|倭文造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidori-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.

== Legends and Deeds ==

Takehazuchi no Kami appears in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' under the name {{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建葉槌命|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}. She is said to have '''subjugated the star deity''' '''[[:en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Amatsu-Mikaboshi]] (also known as Kagaseo no Kami)''', whom '''{{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1502690}}''' and '''[[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi-no-Kami]]''' were unable to pacify.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author=植松安|date=1920-06|title=仮名日本書紀|volume=上巻|pages=87–88|publisher=大同館書店|url={{NDLDC|961006/117}} |doi=10.11501/961006 }} ({{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国立国会図書館|1=National Diet Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q477675}})</ref>

==Descendant clans==

{{ill|Nagahata clan|ja|長幡部連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahata clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shizuri clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tanabe clan|ja|田辺宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabe clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takehara clan|ja|竹原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Okura clan|ja|巨椋連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okura clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja|大椋置始連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Okihajime clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Imaki clan|ja|今木連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tame clan|ja|多米連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tame clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Minu clan|ja|美努連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Minokuni Rulers|ja|三野県主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minokuni Rulers|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Tottoribe clan|ja|鳥取部連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tottoribe clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Jingu clan|ja|神宮部造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Agatanoinukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agatanoinukai clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai clan|qq=}}

== Shrines ==
{{ill|Takehazuchi|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|simple|User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} is believed to be the ancestor of the {{Nihongo|Shitori clan|倭文氏}} of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}}<ref name="Yoshiki" />.<ref name="mizue-2" /> 

However, the legends of {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|qq=}} are all centered around {{ill|Shitateruhime|lt=Shitateruhime-no-Mikoto|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}}, whom the shrine legend claims arrived in this location from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-taisha|qq=}} by boat. She established her residence on the mountain behind this shrine, and after her death, she was buried in a ''{{ill|kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}}'' constructed in the shrine's precincts.<ref name="Yoshiki">{{cite book|last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese|qq=draft}}</ref>
